req.title: Appointment scheduling (boundary)
req.statement: A patient shall schedule an appointment at least 24 hours in advance during provider's available slots. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
error_pathways: 422 when slot unavailable or in past

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-049 mandates a 24-hour advance booking rule against provider-available slots. The critical boundaries are: exactly 24 h in advance (valid), exactly 24 h minus 1 second (invalid), and a slot well inside the future (nominal valid). Equivalence partitions are: future slots ≥24 h (valid), slots <24 h or past (invalid). State transitions cover the appointment lifecycle: unauthenticated → authenticated → slot-selected → confirmed. A decision table captures the cross-product of slot-availability × lead-time. The violation in test[5] was heuristic=STC on a negative kind — STC is reserved for state-transition coverage which is a structural/positive traversal heuristic; the unavailable-slot scenario is a decision-table negative row, so heuristic is corrected to DT. All tests hit the REST API at /appointments, are idempotent via teardown fixtures, and use ISO-8601 timestamps computed at runtime relative to utcnow() to remain deterministic.

tests.name: test_schedule_appointment_48h_in_advance_succeeds
preconditions: Patient is authenticated (valid JWT in session)
Provider has an available slot 48 hours from now
No existing appointment occupies that slot
actions: Compute slot_start = utcnow() + 48 h, rounded to next provider slot boundary
Capture HTTP status and response body
trace: R-HLT-049: appointment created when slot is available and ≥24 h in advance
R-HLT-049: provider notified on successful booking
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_appointment_48h_in_advance_succeeds(
    auth_client,        # <fixture>: returns authenticated requests.Session
    provider_slot,      # <fixture>: ensures provider slot exists, yields slot_id
    cleanup_appointment # <fixture>: deletes created appointment after test
):
    slot_start = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=48)
    ).replace(minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0).isoformat()

    payload = {
        "patient_id": "<test_patient_id>",
        "provider_id": "<test_provider_id>",
        "slot_start": slot_start,
    }

    resp = auth_client.post("/appointments", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "appointment_id" in body
    assert body.get("status") == "booked"

    notification = <helper_fn>.get_notification(body["appointment_id"])
    assert notification is not None, "Provider notification not recorded"

    cleanup_appointment(body["appointment_id"])

tests.name: test_schedule_appointment_less_than_24h_returns_422
preconditions: Patient is authenticated (valid JWT in session)
A provider slot exists 12 hours from now (less than 24 h lead time)
actions: Capture HTTP status and error body
expected: Error body contains code indicating slot is in the past or inside 24-h window
trace: R-HLT-049: error pathway — 422 when slot is within 24-h advance window
R-HLT-049: appointment must NOT be created
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_appointment_less_than_24h_returns_422(
    auth_client,   # <fixture>
    provider_slot  # <fixture>
):
    slot_start = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=12)
    ).replace(minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0).isoformat()

    payload = {
        "patient_id": "<test_patient_id>",
        "provider_id": "<test_provider_id>",
        "slot_start": slot_start,
    }

    resp = auth_client.post("/appointments", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, "Response must contain error detail"
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert any(k in error_text for k in ("advance", "24", "past", "unavailable"))

tests.name: test_schedule_appointment_exactly_24h_minus_1s_returns_422
preconditions: Patient is authenticated (valid JWT in session)
A provider slot exists at exactly utcnow() + 24 h - 1 s
actions: Compute slot_start = utcnow() + 24 h - 1 second (ISO-8601 with seconds)
expected: Booking is rejected because timestamp is strictly less than the 24-h threshold
trace: R-HLT-049: boundary — one second below the 24-h advance rule must be rejected
R-HLT-049: boundary value analysis on timestamp numeric element
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_appointment_exactly_24h_minus_1s_returns_422(
    auth_client,   # <fixture>
    provider_slot  # <fixture>
):
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    slot_start = (now + timedelta(hours=24) - timedelta(seconds=1)).isoformat()

    payload = {
        "patient_id": "<test_patient_id>",
        "provider_id": "<test_provider_id>",
        "slot_start": slot_start,
    }

    resp = auth_client.post("/appointments", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422, (
        f"BVA lower bound (24h-1s) must be rejected; got {resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_schedule_appointment_exactly_24h_in_advance_succeeds
preconditions: Patient is authenticated (valid JWT in session)
Provider has a slot available at exactly utcnow() + 24 h
actions: Compute slot_start = utcnow() + 24 h exactly (ISO-8601 with seconds)
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Response body contains appointment_id and status = 'booked'
Exactly-24-h boundary is treated as valid
trace: R-HLT-049: boundary — exactly 24 h in advance is the minimum valid value
R-HLT-049: boundary value analysis on timestamp numeric element
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_appointment_exactly_24h_in_advance_succeeds(
    auth_client,        # <fixture>
    provider_slot,      # <fixture>
    cleanup_appointment # <fixture>
):
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    slot_start = (now + timedelta(hours=24)).isoformat()

    payload = {
        "patient_id": "<test_patient_id>",
        "provider_id": "<test_provider_id>",
        "slot_start": slot_start,
    }

    resp = auth_client.post("/appointments", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"BVA exact lower bound (24h) must be accepted; got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "booked"
    cleanup_appointment(body["appointment_id"])

tests.name: test_schedule_appointment_unavailable_slot_returns_422
preconditions: Patient is authenticated (valid JWT in session)
Target slot is 48 h from now but already booked by another patient
actions: Pre-book the target slot via helper_fn.force_book_slot(slot_id)
Attempt to POST /appointments for the same slot_id
Capture HTTP status and error body
expected: Error body indicates slot is unavailable
trace: R-HLT-049: error pathway — 422 when slot is unavailable
R-HLT-049: decision table row: lead_time=OK, availability=TAKEN => reject
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_appointment_unavailable_slot_returns_422(
    auth_client,          # <fixture>
    provider_slot,        # <fixture>: yields slot_id for a slot 48 h from now
    force_book_slot,      # <fixture/helper>: pre-books the given slot_id
    cleanup_appointment   # <fixture>: cleans up the pre-booked appointment
):
    slot_id = provider_slot
    pre_booked_id = force_book_slot(slot_id)

    slot_start = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=48)
    ).replace(minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0).isoformat()

    payload = {
        "patient_id": "<test_patient_id_2>",
        "provider_id": "<test_provider_id>",
        "slot_start": slot_start,
        "slot_id": slot_id,
    }

    resp = auth_client.post("/appointments", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for taken slot, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert any(k in error_text for k in ("unavailable", "taken", "conflict", "booked"))

    cleanup_appointment(pre_booked_id)

tests.name: test_schedule_appointment_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: No authentication token is present in the session
Provider has an available slot 48 hours from now
actions: Capture HTTP status and error body
expected: Response body contains authentication error detail
trace: R-HLT-049: precondition — only authenticated patients may book
R-HLT-049: unauthenticated request must be rejected before slot logic is evaluated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_appointment_unauthenticated_returns_401(
    anon_client  # <fixture>: returns unauthenticated requests.Session
):
    slot_start = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=48)
    ).replace(minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0).isoformat()

    payload = {
        "patient_id": "<test_patient_id>",
        "provider_id": "<test_provider_id>",
        "slot_start": slot_start,
    }

    resp = anon_client.post("/appointments", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401 for unauthenticated request, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
